description:
You are a system administrator performing a quick security audit on an SSH
    authentication log.  
    Everything you need is already on the machine:

    • The raw log file is at  
      /home/user/logs/auth.log  

    Your goal is to build a very small report, using regular-expression based
    filtering, and place every file you create inside the new directory  
    /home/user/filtered_logs.

    Steps the automated grader will check:

    1. Directory creation  
       • /home/user/filtered_logs must exist when you finish.

    2. Regex filtering (case sensitive)  
       • Extract every line whose message text contains the exact substring  
         “Failed password” and write them, in their original order, to  
         /home/user/filtered_logs/failed.log  
       • Extract every line whose message text contains the exact substring  
         “Accepted password” and write them, in their original order, to  
         /home/user/filtered_logs/accepted.log  

    3. Summary file  
       • Create /home/user/filtered_logs/summary.log containing exactly two
         lines, in this precise order and format (including the terminating
         newline on the second line):

           Failed: <N>
           Accepted: <M>

         where  
         <N> is the total number of lines inside failed.log and  
         <M> is the total number of lines inside accepted.log.

    4. Console output (for the person watching)  
       • After the summary file is produced, print the same two lines to STDOUT
         so a human observer can see the counts immediately.

    Important formatting rules the grader will enforce:

    • failed.log and accepted.log must preserve the original log lines exactly
      (byte-for-byte); no extra spaces, headers or footers.  
    • summary.log must contain exactly the two lines shown above, each ending
      with a single ‘\n’.  
    • The counts in summary.log must match the actual number of lines present
      in the corresponding filtered files.

    Complexity guidance: The entire task can be completed with roughly 5-10
    shell commands (mkdir, grep/egrep, wc, echo, etc.).  Feel free to use any
    combination of standard GNU/Linux command-line tools as long as the final
    filesystem state matches everything described above.
truth:
Pre-existing file provided to the agent:

    • /home/user/logs/auth.log  
      (10 lines, content shown below including terminating newlines)

        Jan 10 09:00:01 server sshd[12345]: Accepted password for alice from 192.168.1.10 port 53422 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:02:15 server sshd[12346]: Failed password for invalid user root from 203.0.113.1 port 61123 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:05:47 server sshd[12347]: Failed password for bob from 192.168.1.11 port 51234 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:07:32 server sshd[12348]: Accepted password for charlie from 192.168.1.12 port 55211 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:10:09 server sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.2 port 62345 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:15:55 server sshd[12350]: Accepted password for alice from 192.168.1.10 port 53445 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:20:16 server sshd[12351]: Failed password for dave from 192.168.1.13 port 50001 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:25:45 server sshd[12352]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.3 port 63211 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:30:12 server sshd[12353]: Accepted password for bob from 192.168.1.11 port 51333 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:35:24 server sshd[12354]: Received disconnect from 192.168.1.12 port 55211:11: disconnected by user

    Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    • Directory  
      /home/user/filtered_logs/   (regular directory, default 0755)

    • /home/user/filtered_logs/failed.log  
      (5 lines, byte-for-byte copies of the 2nd, 3rd, 5th, 7th and 8th lines above)

        Jan 10 09:02:15 server sshd[12346]: Failed password for invalid user root from 203.0.113.1 port 61123 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:05:47 server sshd[12347]: Failed password for bob from 192.168.1.11 port 51234 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:10:09 server sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.2 port 62345 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:20:16 server sshd[12351]: Failed password for dave from 192.168.1.13 port 50001 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:25:45 server sshd[12352]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.3 port 63211 ssh2

    • /home/user/filtered_logs/accepted.log  
      (4 lines, byte-for-byte copies of the 1st, 4th, 6th and 9th lines above)

        Jan 10 09:00:01 server sshd[12345]: Accepted password for alice from 192.168.1.10 port 53422 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:07:32 server sshd[12348]: Accepted password for charlie from 192.168.1.12 port 55211 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:15:55 server sshd[12350]: Accepted password for alice from 192.168.1.10 port 53445 ssh2
        Jan 10 09:30:12 server sshd[12353]: Accepted password for bob from 192.168.1.11 port 51333 ssh2

    • /home/user/filtered_logs/summary.log  
      (exactly 2 lines, each ending in ‘\n’)

        Failed: 5
        Accepted: 4

    • The agent must also have printed the following two lines to STDOUT at some
      point (whitespace identical):

        Failed: 5
        Accepted: 4
